response.justification: The candidate brings genuine high-throughput systems experience (microservices handling millions of requests per second with low-latency async I/O at Dunnhumby), strong TypeScript, and directly relevant current work on AI-agent infrastructure including MCP code generators and agentic workflow tooling at Naver. However, Python — a must-have listed as 'strong' — is entirely absent from the resume, the deepest systems work was done in DLang (a niche stack), and despite Staff titles there is little concrete evidence of directing technical strategy, cross-functional stakeholder management, or operational/incident ownership at staff scope. Worth a conversation given the AI-native alignment, but with clear gaps to probe.
strengths: Hands-on high-throughput, low-latency distributed systems experience (millions of HTTP requests/sec)
Current work building AI-agent infrastructure, MCP code generators, and agentic workflow frameworks
Strong TypeScript/Node.js plus deep testing and software-craftsmanship discipline (TDD, CI/CD, trunk-based)
concerns: No Python experience listed, which the role requires at a 'strong' level
Little demonstrated staff-level technical strategy, stakeholder coordination, or on-call/incident ownership
Deepest systems work is in DLang and dated (ended 2020); recent roles skew toward full-stack product work
